seoTitle: How to Export Your n8n Workflow (Backup Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to export an n8n workflow as a JSON file to back it up, share it with your team, or migrate it to another environment.
intro: Exporting a workflow turns your automation into a portable JSON file you can back up, share with teammates, or move into a different n8n environment. This guide covers downloading a full workflow blueprint, copying specific node selections, and what does and doesn't come along with the export.

steps.heading: Open the workflow you want to back up
steps.body: Load the specific automation canvas you plan to export from your n8n dashboard.

steps.heading: Open the file operations menu
steps.body: Click the options settings icon to reveal the file management dropdown.

steps.heading: Download the workflow as JSON
steps.body: Select the download action to convert the entire automation tree into a structured JSON file.

steps.heading: Save it somewhere safe
steps.body: Store the exported file in a local backup folder or a shared drive so you can restore it later if needed.

steps.heading: Optionally copy just a section
steps.body: You can also select specific node groups on the canvas and copy their raw JSON to your clipboard instead of exporting the whole workflow.

steps.heading: Verify the file before migrating
steps.body: Open the exported JSON to confirm the structure looks complete before using it in a different environment.

faq.question: Does exporting a workflow include my credentials?
faq.answer: No, sensitive account credentials are kept separate from the exported JSON, so anyone importing the file will need to set up their own authentication.

faq.question: Can I export just part of a workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select specific nodes on the canvas and copy their JSON to your clipboard instead of downloading the entire workflow.

faq.question: What file format does n8n export to?
faq.answer: Workflows are exported as standard .json files, which makes them easy to version, share, or store alongside other project files.

faq.question: Should I back up workflows before a version upgrade?
faq.answer: Yes, exporting your active workflows before major upgrades or workspace cleanups is a good safety habit.
